title: Define a Struct
instruction: Define a `Person` struct with `Name string` and `Age int`. Create a Person with name "Alice" and age 30, then print both fields on separate lines.
hint: type Person struct { Name string; Age int } — p := Person{Name: "Alice", Age: 30}

title: Struct Method (Value Receiver)
instruction: Define a `Greet()` method on `Person` that returns `"Hi, " + p.Name + "!"`. Create Alice and print the result of calling her `Greet()` method.
hint: func (p Person) Greet() string { return "Hi, " + p.Name + "!" }

title: Nested Structs
instruction: Define an `Address` struct with `City string`. Add an `Address` field to `Person`. Create a Person in London and print the city.
hint: type Address struct { City string }; type Person struct { Name string; Address Address }

title: Anonymous Struct
instruction: Create an anonymous struct inline with `Name` and `Score` fields, set them to "Bob" and 95, and print both.
hint: entry := struct{ Name string; Score int }{Name: "Bob", Score: 95}

title: Pointer to Struct
instruction: Use `new(Person)` to allocate a Person, set the Name field to "Charlie", then print the name.
